prompt: Photograph of a contractor on a residential job site — three-quarter back angle, face mostly obscured by the angle and the brim of a worn cap. Wearing a faded blue flannel shirt over a charcoal henley, broken-in dark denim jeans with a smear of joint compound, scuffed brown work boots. Holding a 25-foot Stanley tape measure with a small saturated red end-cap (the only saturated red in the frame). A worn leather tool belt slung at the waist with a hammer in the loop. Standing inside a half-finished room with exposed studs and plywood subfloor catching late-afternoon golden light through a bare window. Sawdust drifting in the light shaft. 35mm prime, shallow depth of field, warm tungsten + window-mix lighting, slightly desaturated palette except the tape-measure red. High-contrast working-contractor catalog feel, NOT HGTV bright, NOT luxury staged. The subject's face is intentionally not the focal point — composition emphasizes the hands, tools, and the room.
